title: Poincianella oblongifolia
intro: Poincianella oblongifolia, also known as P. pyramidalis, is a tree species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Poincianella oblongifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Poincianella oblongifolia is classified in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, which includes over 3,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Poincianella is part of the tribe Cassieae. Within the Cassieae tribe, Poincianella oblongifolia is closely related to other species of Poincianella and Cassia. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships.

side_effects: Poincianella oblongifolia, like other plants in the family Fabaceae, may cause side effects when consumed due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large quantities can lead to gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Some people may also experience allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, or anaphylaxis in severe cases. Poincianella oblongifolia has been reported to contain phytohemagglutinin, a toxic compound that can cause hemolytic anemia. It is essential to handle and consume this plant with caution and under proper medical guidance.
